paper_title: External Auditory Exostoses in Pleistocene and Recent Human Samples

methods.description: This method involves identifying and grading external auditory exostoses (EAE) in Pleistocene specimens and recent human samples to assess the presence and severity of EAE.

steps.description: Paleontological observations have been made visually on twenty-nine original Pleistocene specimens, by us and/or the paleontologists who have commented on presence/absence of EAE in individual specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: collect visual observations of EAE from Pleistocene specimens

steps.description: When high quality (resin) casts are available, they have been employed. Otherwise, the additional observations are based on high resolution digital scans of the temporal bones or detailed photographs.
steps.searchable_summary: use resin casts, digital scans, or photographs for EAE observation

steps.description: The grade scores allotted in the study were checked by all authors and discussed before reaching a final agreement.
steps.searchable_summary: review and finalize EAE grade scores

steps.description: If the EAE appears intermediate between two grades, it was counted conservatively as the lower grade.
steps.searchable_summary: assign conservative EAE grade for intermediate cases

methods.description: Statistical methods used to compare EAE presence and severity across different human samples and latitudinal distributions.

steps.description: Exact Chi-square and binomial p-values were computed using StatXact 4.0.1 for the latitudinal distributions of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Chi-square and binomial p-values for latitudinal distributions

steps.description: Wilcoxon / Mann-Whitney p-values were determined using NCSS 11.0.19 for the latitudinal distributions of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Wilcoxon/Mann-Whitney p-values for latitudinal distributions

steps.description: Binomial 95% confidence intervals for the fossil sample EAE percentages derive from the online calculator of Soper.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate binomial 95% confidence intervals for EAE percentages
